title: All About Me
text:
“Versatile business professional with a creative mindset, proven adaptability, and a drive to deliver results in fast-paced environments.”
I am an MSc Business Analytics student at DCU and a Trainee Accountant at the Mercantile Group in Dublin. Over the past three years I have been building a career that bridges two worlds — financial accountability and data-driven decision making.
I believe the most valuable professionals in modern business are those who can read the numbers and understand the story behind them. That is what I am working towards.

title: Ruairí Cullen
text: Like to have fun!!!

title: Consumer Complaint Analytics — Bank of America
text:
This project transforms Bank of America’s complaint management from a slow reactive First-In-First-Out system into an AI-powered fast-track routing system. Using 61,022 real complaints from the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB), a Voting Classifier combining Histogram-based Gradient Boosting and Random Forest achieves 86.1% precision identifying complaints likely to require monetary relief.






Tip



🚀 Live Dashboard: analysisofcomplaints.streamlit.app

title: Consumer Complaint Analytics — Bank of America
text:
This project transforms Bank of America’s complaint management from a slow reactive First-In-First-Out system into an AI-powered fast-track routing system. Using 61,022 real complaints from the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB), a Voting Classifier combining Histogram-based Gradient Boosting and Random Forest achieves 86.1% precision identifying complaints likely to require monetary relief.






Tip



🚀 Live Dashboard: analysisofcomplaints.streamlit.app

title: Consumer Complaint Analytics — Bank of America
text:
Key Results



Metric
Result




Model
Voting Classifier (Gradient Boosting + Random Forest)


Precision
86.1%


ROC-AUC
0.794


Accuracy
77.4%


Dataset Size
61,022 CFPB complaints


Projected Gross Savings
$510,000


Operational Cost
$39,500


Net Benefit
$470,500


ROI
1,191%

title: Consumer Complaint Analytics — Bank of America
text:
Feature Engineering
The pipeline engineered the following features from raw CFPB data:

TF-IDF vectorisation of complaint Issue text (unigrams + bigrams, 1,000 features)
Temporal features — Days_to_Forward, Month_Sin, Month_Cos, Day_of_Week, Quarter
Risk rate features — Product_Risk_Rate, State_Risk_Rate (historical payout probabilities)
Text metadata — Issue_Length, Issue_Word_Count

Show imports
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

# Unified colour theme
THEME = {
    'primary': '#4C9AFF',
    'secondary': '#A78BFA',
    'success': '#34D399',
    'danger': '#F87171',
    'warning': '#FBBF24',
    'neutral': '#475569',
}
print("Libraries loaded.")


Libraries loaded.

title: Consumer Complaint Analytics — Bank of America
text:
Python Code — Model Performance Visualisation

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

metrics = ['Precision', 'ROC-AUC', 'Accuracy', 'Recall']
values  = [86.1, 79.4, 77.4, 4.6]
colors  = ['#4C9AFF', '#A78BFA', '#34D399', '#F87171']

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 4))
bars = ax.barh(metrics, values, color=colors, edgecolor='white', height=0.5)
ax.set_xlim(0, 105)
ax.set_xlabel('Score (%)', fontsize=11)
ax.set_title('Voting Classifier — Model Performance Metrics',
             fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', pad=15)
ax.axvline(x=80, color='grey', linestyle='--', alpha=0.5, label='80% benchmark')
for bar, val in zip(bars, values):
    ax.text(val + 1, bar.get_y() + bar.get_height() / 2,
            f'{val}%', va='center', fontweight='bold', fontsize=10)
ax.legend(fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()




Voting Classifier performance metrics on the CFPB test set

title: Consumer Complaint Analytics — Bank of America
text:
Python Code — Financial Simulation

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Simulation parameters (from report)
COST_PER_REVIEW   = 50
AVG_PAYOUT_SAVED  = 750
flagged_cases     = 1020

thresholds    = np.arange(0.3, 0.8, 0.05)
gross_savings = [flagged_cases * AVG_PAYOUT_SAVED * (t * 0.9) for t in thresholds]
op_costs      = [flagged_cases * COST_PER_REVIEW * (1 - t * 0.3) for t in thresholds]
net_benefit   = [g - o for g, o in zip(gross_savings, op_costs)]
roi           = [(n / o) * 100 for n, o in zip(net_benefit, op_costs)]

fig, ax1 = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 4))
ax2 = ax1.twinx()

ax1.plot(thresholds, net_benefit, 'b-o', linewidth=2, label='Net Benefit ($)')
ax1.set_xlabel('Risk Threshold', fontsize=11)
ax1.set_ylabel('Net Benefit ($)', color='blue', fontsize=11)
ax1.tick_params(axis='y', labelcolor='blue')

ax2.plot(thresholds, roi, 'g--s', linewidth=2, label='ROI (%)')
ax2.set_ylabel('ROI (%)', color='green', fontsize=11)
ax2.tick_params(axis='y', labelcolor='green')

ax1.axvline(x=0.5, color='red', linestyle=':', alpha=0.6, label='Selected threshold (0.50)')
ax1.set_title('Financial Impact by Risk Threshold', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold', pad=15)
fig.legend(loc='upper right', bbox_to_anchor=(0.88, 0.88), fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()




Net benefit and ROI across different risk thresholds

title: Consumer Complaint Analytics — Bank of America
text:
Ethical Considerations






All Personally Identifiable Information (PII) was removed prior to analysis. Sensitive demographic variables — race, gender, and exact location — were excluded to ensure fair, bias-free predictions based solely on complaint content and product metadata.
